mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe earnings headwinds, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, invalidating the threshold signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal savings and housing starts indicates robust household balance sheets and capital expenditure, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window, or housing starts fail to correlate with equity returns when savings rise.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month rebounds in industrial production during crisis troughs signal supply chain normalization, triggering short-term positive equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns remain negative or flat during industrial production rebounds, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over the recovery period.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payroll employment reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a rank IC above 0.03 across multiple expansion cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 240.0, accelerating inflation forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL breaches the 240.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in real GDP and surges in personal savings indicate forced consumption suppression, creating negative earnings revisions and downward equity pressure over one month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP decline and savings surge signal produces positive forward returns in more than half of observed quarters, breaking the causal link.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment during normalization phases confirms sustainable demand recovery, driving positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Five-day returns turn negative when industrial production rises alongside unemployment declines, with a negative rank IC over the recovery window.
